title: S7: LAN-IP reachability + deploy/procd wiring
description: Make the front desk reachable by the node's LAN gateway IP independent of .mesh resolution (that is e21.1's job). procd service for mjolnir-hello in the OpenWrt deploy; /etc/config/mjolnir UCI flags (--directory-file/--spool-dir/--bind); health-gate integration. AC: hello reachable at http://<node-lan-ip>/ with meshd running; starts under procd; optional (mesh runs without it); survives the deploy health gate. [test_tier: smoke] Depends on bl2.

title: S5: frontend directory page (mesh-wide neighbors/identities/services)
description: Render the directory from /api/directory: mesh-wide neighbors, identities, and services (each service with name/addr/port), plus a 'you are here' node header; poll for updates. AC: remote-origin identities and services render (not just local node); a new node/identity appears within ~15s of a poll; page works with an empty/degraded API (graceful). [test_tier: smoke] Depends on gad (scaffold) + S3 (read API).

title: S6: frontend identity affordances (anonymous + soft rung-1 key)
description: Anonymous 'just browse' path (no identity). 'Create an identity' generates an @noble/ed25519 keypair (pure JS — plain HTTP is an insecure context, no WebCrypto), stores it in IndexedDB, fetches a challenge, signs, POSTs to /api/identity. Persistent honesty label: soft custody, extractable by the serving node, not equal to app/hardware custody. AC: anonymous needs no identity; a created identity persists across reload; submission succeeds and the identity later appears in the directory; soft-custody label always visible. [test_tier: smoke] Depends on gad + S4.

title: S4: mjolnir-hello identity API (/api/challenge, POST /api/identity)
description: GET /api/challenge returns a single-use nonce; POST /api/identity validates the submitted Ed25519 signature over the issued challenge and, on success, writes pending/{pubkey}.json to the spool. AC: challenge is single-use; a valid submission is spooled; an invalid signature is rejected with 4xx and nothing spooled; server holds no private key. [test_tier: smoke] Depends on bl2.

title: S3: mjolnir-hello read-only API (/api/directory, /api/node)
description: Serve GET /api/directory and GET /api/node as JSON, sourced by reading the daemon-written directory.json (stat-poll + short TTL cache; do NOT re-derive mesh state). AC: /api/directory returns node+neighbors+identities+services from directory.json; /api/node returns this node's summary; stale file tolerated with last-good; schema version surfaced. [test_tier: smoke] Depends on avs (directory.json) + bl2 (scaffold).

title: Gossip bootstrap union: rejoin from roster ∪ persisted addrbook ∪ claim owners (self-healing joins, no more islands)
description: Follow-up to the 0yb rollout island: gossip join/rejoin uses ONLY the static UCI/roster peer ids (mjolnir-meshd.rs run_mesh ~L729, gossip_rejoin_loop), so a node whose configured peers are all down stays a gossip island even when other live, dialable nodes are known. Fix: compute the bootstrap candidate set as the union of (1) roster/UCI peers, (2) persisted addrbook.state node ids (0yb — a node that ever converged re-bootstraps from what it learned), (3) persisted claims.state owner_node_ids with peer_backhaul_hint for addresses; optionally (4) mDNS-discovered node ids as link-local bootstrap. Recompute the union on each zero-neighbor rejoin retry so entries learned mid-run get used. Derived-address seeding into the daemon MemoryLookup already covers dialing. CAUTION: cap candidates per attempt / prefer recently-seen so a large old fleet doesn't turn each retry into a dial storm. Complements m4a (provisioning renders full peer sets — first join); this makes every subsequent join self-healing. Cannot help a factory-fresh node with an empty disk and stale roster (met enrollment territory).

title: Spike: gossip one new CRDT record type (/users) end-to-end across two nodes
description: De-risk the hello.mesh MVP critical path FIRST. Prove that a new record type (a /users map entry) written at node A propagates over the existing gossip/CRDT layer and is observable at node B — reusing the validated transport that already converges subnet claims mesh-wide. Smallest possible slice: define the record, gossip it, read it at the peer. Success = a value set at A is read at B within seconds over 802.11s. Informs FR22/FR28 and the service-record slice (FR20). Blocks the hello.mesh epic's propagation work.
design: SPIKE RESULT (validated): a new /users CRDT record type propagates end-to-end over the existing GossipSync seam. Pattern (mirrors peer_addr/subnet): (1) UserEntry struct + UserBook=BTreeMap in crdt/users.rs, keyed by username, HLC updated_at; (2) GossipMessage::UserUpdate{username,entry} appended last (old nodes decode-skip); (3) merge_user() pure LWW on updated_at, no conflict arm (any node may write a user, unlike peer_addr's sole-announcer); (4) lib.rs re-exports. Proof: tests/users_gossip_e2e.rs stands up two GossipSync nodes over an in-process paired transport, A publishes UserUpdate, B's real recv->decode->merge->store loop converges within seconds; also proves LWW update + stale-discard. Encode/decode/dispatch machinery is the same code the iroh transport runs. NOT YET proven on physical 802.11s (follow-up fleet validation, as 0yb got). De-risks FR22/FR28 + service-record slice FR20; unblocks p6u (ingest identity submissions into gossip) and daemon wiring (apply UserUpdate to a UserBook + anti-entropy re-broadcast, both trivial adds). NOTE: daemon feature currently unbuildable on main for an unrelated reason -> bead hg0.
notes: VALIDATED 2026-07-04 (commit ed011d2). Built crdt/users.rs (UserEntry+UserBook @ /users/{username}), GossipMessage::UserUpdate (appended last, decode-skip discipline), merge_user() pure LWW, and tests/users_gossip_e2e.rs (two real GossipSync over in-process paired transport: publish->recv->decode->merge->store converges; LWW update + stale-discard proven). 46 crdt / 125 lib tests pass, clippy clean. NOT yet on physical 802.11s (see field-validation follow-up). Drove the identical encode/decode/dispatch the iroh transport runs.
close_reason: Closed

title: meshd seam: ingest identity submissions from pending/ spool into gossip
description: meshd watches/sweeps the pending/ spool that mjolnir-hello writes, validates each identity submission's signature, writes it into /users gossip, and removes the file. Keeps mjolnir-hello a thin file-shuffler (no IPC). AC: a spooled VALID submission enters /users and propagates mesh-wide (see S1); an invalid submission is dropped; the file is removed after ingest; duplicate submissions are idempotent. [test_tier: thorough] Depends on S1 (/users record type).
notes: PROMOTED to MVP/critical-path 2026-07-04: mesh-wide identity propagation is the product's headline (a friend coming online across the mesh), not Growth. Rides the field-validated gossip transport (subnet claims already converge mesh-wide incl cross-site) — work is /users record type + spool ingest + directory wiring, not new transport. See PRD docs/products/hello.mesh/prd.md FR22/FR28.

title: meshd seam: write read-only directory.json projection of gossip snapshot
description: meshd periodically writes a versioned read-only directory.json = {version, node, neighbors (from AddrBook + subnet claims), identities (from /users), services (from ServiceEntry registry)}, atomic replace (same pattern as persist_claims/persist_addr_book). AC: valid JSON with a schema version field; reflects a node/identity/service change within the write cadence; atomic (no torn reads); path configurable. [test_tier: thorough] Depends on S1 (/users) + S2 (services) for the identity/service sections; neighbors available today.

title: crates/mjolnir-hello: server binary (embedded static + read-only JSON API)
description: New workspace crate crates/mjolnir-hello + binary. Depends on the mjolnir-mesh LIBRARY (iroh-free) for CRDT types. Serves the embedded SvelteKit bundle via rust-embed; HTTP via tiny_http; GET /api/health; config via /etc/config/mjolnir UCI + flags; procd service. AC: binary builds standalone with NO iroh dependency; serves the embedded bundle at /; /api/health returns liveness; runs independently of meshd (mesh works without it). [test_tier: smoke]

title: hello.mesh frontend: SvelteKit SSG bundle (directory + soft rung-1 identity)
description: SvelteKit + adapter-static (prerender=true) → pure static bundle, NO external hosts (offline-capable, CSP-clean). Build output feeds the rust-embed pipeline in mjolnir-hello. This is the SCAFFOLD story (directory page = S5, identity = S6). AC: builds to static files only; loads and runs with zero internet; references no external host; produces the bundle mjolnir-hello embeds. [test_tier: smoke]

title: [epic] hello.mesh front desk: static frontend + server + read-only mesh API
description: Build the hello.mesh front desk per docs/network-coordination/hello-mesh-service.md. Three components + two daemon seams: (1) SvelteKit SSG static frontend (adapter-static, noble-ed25519 for soft rung-1 keys in insecure context), (2) new crates/mjolnir-hello binary serving embedded static bundle (rust-embed) + read-only JSON API (tiny_http), (3) REST API over mesh state. Daemon seams: read = meshd writes read-only directory.json projection of gossip snapshot; write = mjolnir-hello spools identity submissions to pending/ for meshd ingest. DNS/.mesh resolution is a SEPARATE track (design stops at the seam, §5). Demo-oriented: neighbor directory from real data available today + anonymous + soft rung-1 identity. Parent spec: rp9 (user-identity).
notes: SPRINT-001 EXEC NOTE (2026-07-04): spike 2xd DONE — /users record type, gossip, LWW merge, e2e all landed in the lib (commit ed011d2); S1/zhg shrunk to daemon-wiring-only. BUILD CONSTRAINT (hg0): the mjolnir-meshd daemon is Linux-only (deep rtnetlink/tun deps) — daemon-track stories (zhg wiring, S2 wiring, avs, p6u) build/test on Linux or via deploy/openwrt/build.sh; their record/merge/projection LOGIC lives in the lib and is Mac-testable. Server (mjolnir-hello) + frontend build on any host (lib is iroh-free). hg0 is a macOS-dev papercut, NOT a deploy blocker and NOT the rename its report claimed. Field-validation of /users on physical 802.11s tracked separately.

title: Fleet peer-list provisioning: install-node/update-fleet should render FULL peer sets (chain topology caused a gossip island)
description: Found during 0yb validation 2026-07-03: each node's /etc/config/mjolnir had exactly ONE 'list peer' (wr3000s-a→m3000-b, m3000→tr3000, tr3000→m3000). With m3000-b offline, wr3000s-a became a permanent gossip island (data plane fine — babel routed; CRDT plane dead: no claims, no addrbook). Manually flattened to full peer sets on all live nodes. Follow-up: derive each node's peer list from fleet-nodes.conf during install/update so bootstrap survives any single-node outage; longer-term the addrbook (0yb) + enrollment (met) subsume hand-kept lists.

title: Backhaul address collision detection + re-derivation via claim CRDT
description: From the bsa decision (docs/network-coordination/ipv6-addressing-decision.md): the derived backhaul address 10.254.<blake3(node_id)[0..2]>/16 has 16 bits of entropy — ~50% birthday-collision odds at ~300 nodes, currently with NO resolution protocol (silent duplicate address). Fix with the existing claim-CRDT machinery: (1) gossip backhaul addresses as claims (/backhaul/{addr}) on the existing 20s anti-entropy loop; (2) on Conflict{winner,loser} merge verdict, the HLC loser re-derives deterministically via blake3(node_id || counter), rewrites its claim, reconfigures br-mesh; (3) optional headroom: widen the pool (100.64.0.0/10 CGN → 23 bits, ~50% at ~3400 nodes) as config. Same shape as losing a /24 claim — reuse merge.rs, no new consensus. Chosen over IPv6 migration (rejected in bsa).
notes: FORCED-COLLISION VALIDATED 2026-07-02 (local 3-node Docker harness, aarch64 static binary ad6bbc7, alpine arm64 + NET_ADMIN, respawn-loop as procd stand-in). Brute-forced two real ed25519 identities colliding on 10.254.154.104 (401 keygens — right at the birthday estimate for 16 bits); third normal node relayed gossip (the realistic fleet path). Observed: both twins claim the addr; ~19s later (one anti-entropy relay) the HLC-later twin logs 'backhaul address collision lost — restarting to re-derive (pt9)', exits 86 EXACTLY ONCE, respawns, re-derives 10.254.148.140 (attempt 1), publishes the new /32; winner never restarts; third node converges all 4 claims (2x /32 + both client /24s); no flapping, no livelock. Test harness: scratchpad pt9-collision-test.sh + collide brute-forcer. REMAINING for close: fleet rollout (update-fleet.sh) — currently blocked: workstation has no path to the mesh (on the 192.168.0.x upstream LAN; jump node 192.168.1.1 unreachable, wan-side ssh correctly firewalled). ProxyJump stanza pre-staged in ~/.ssh/config; clean-stamped binary built. Deploy is one command once the operator joins the mesh wifi or wires into a node LAN port.
close_reason: FIELD-VALIDATED and closed. Fleet rollout 2026-07-02 (build 47ec70c, update-fleet.sh, in-band over the mesh, all 4 nodes, zero rollbacks): every node's effective backhaul address matches the inventory (no false collisions from the salted derivation — attempt 0 is byte-identical to legacy), every node converged all 3 peers' /32 backhaul claims via gossip/anti-entropy, zero collision events, babel routing intact throughout (health gates + post-checks passed; transient 0-neighbour reading on m3000-b settled to 4 within seconds). Collision path itself proven in the local 3-node Docker harness with two brute-forced genuinely-colliding ed25519 identities: FWW loser exited 86 exactly once, respawned, re-derived attempt-1, mesh converged, winner unmoved. Follow-up (noted earlier) still open as design note: overlay-mode ConnManager demux key is attempt-0-derived — make claim-aware if/when overlay ships as default.

title: Spec: IdentiKey user identity + hello.mesh front desk (key-based user auth on the mesh)
description: Extend identity-by-key from nodes to people. Users identified by IdentiKey cryptographic keys on a custody spectrum (anonymous / ephemeral / node-custodied guest badge / custodial / self-custodied); membership and service visibility answered by keys and webs of trust. Enrollment happens at id.mesh — a node-hosted identity surface ('front desk, not checkpoint') discovered via RFC 8910/8908 non-blocking affordance, DNS, and printed QR; NOT a captive portal (captive portals only appear by blocking, and the CNA sandbox is hostile to key material — see spec). True captive behavior survives only as optional per-node gated policy. Makes 'the network is a projection of a set of keys' user-facing. Full spec: docs/network-coordination/user-identity.md. Related: met (node enrollment), e21 (service mesh epic).
design: Full spec: docs/network-coordination/user-identity.md. Decided 2026-07-03: (1) net OPEN, identity unlocks more, anonymous is a supported mode; (2) protocol verifies only signatures, never servers (passkeys rejected per Papyrus SP-02 — RP-ID structurally centralized); (3) NO node ever holds a user private key (invariant) — retired former rung-2 node-custodied; (4) custody spectrum now: 0 anonymous / 1 browser key (SOFT self-custody, WebCrypto non-extractable at the hello.mesh origin) / 3 custodial (OAuth/OIDC to a chosen custodian; identikey-core is one such, never a dependency) / 4 self-custodied app/hardware (HARD); (5) identity = Papyrus attestation chain (identity Ed25519 signs device pubkey, stateless offline verify), consumes identikey-client crate; user records reuse met web-of-trust CRDT shapes; (6) FRONT DESK = hello.mesh, node-hosted landing page per /24: renders live neighbor/service directory (projection of 0yb+e21), does identity, optional gated mode. Single stable name is REQUIRED because same-origin policy locks rung-1 browser keys to one origin → hello.mesh is the OIDC-style IdP origin that vends assertions to other .mesh sites (also gives free roaming persistence). Discovery via RFC 8910/8908 non-blocking affordance + DNS + printed QR, NOT captive interception. Build after 0yb + e21.
notes:
QR DEMOTED 2026-07-04: printed QRs are NOT a dependency. Discovery = RFC 8910/8908 non-blocking affordance + memorable hello.mesh name (a sign saying 'hello.mesh' suffices). QR is optional convenience only, two uses: (1) one-scan WIFI: AP join, (2) out-of-band trust-anchor bootstrap — default first-contact is TOFU over the air (fine: no key yet = nothing to steal), a physically-posted QR is optional high-assurance hardening against a rogue node substituting its EndpointId. On-screen QR/ticket/NFC still used for rung-4 app linking (not printed). No separate printed-guest-QR flow; guest access = open net + one-tap rung-1. See §4.3, §4.4, §4.4a.

Prior notes still hold — DEMO SCOPE (DWeb ~2026-07-06): rung 1 soft pure-JS browser key ('one step beyond anonymous'), hello.mesh directory + anonymous + one-tap identity + publish wiki.mesh from phone. Secure-context reality (rung 1 soft, 1e extension/4 app = hard), no-CA trust ceremony (iroh EndpointId pinning), client→node reputation (trust the graph not a name→key pin; roaming≠evil-twin). Full spec: docs/network-coordination/user-identity.md.

title: Physical-client validation matrix: phone behind far node, AP-hop egress, roaming, resilience
description: Follow-up to apo (closed: cross-mesh client transit validated end-to-end on deployed code, plus 07-01 real-laptop proof on tr3000). Remaining items need a physical client on-site: (1) phone/laptop on wr3000s-a's or m3000's client AP (the FAR side of the mesh) reaching internet + far LANs over the real wifi AP hop; (2) roaming a client between node APs (FT keys are fleet-configured — does the lease/session survive?); (3) resilience: node reboot / radio bounce under an active client flow (a8o failover baseline exists: ~1s withdraw / ~4s restore). Everything is deployed and converged as of 2026-07-02; this is purely bench-time with a device in hand.

title: Link-quality metric: hello-ETX and RTT-under-load both blind to low-MCS radio links on mt76 — needs radio-aware cost
description: Experiment 2026-07-02 (fleet on a657baa, br-mesh type wireless + RTT metric live): tr3000->m3000 link is -80dBm/MCS2/20Mbps while relays exist (via m3000-b: 166+21). (1) Hello-loss ETX: reach bitmask ~bfbf (~12% loss) — multicast hellos ride robust basic rates, cost barely moves. (2) RTT metric under 30s FULL saturation of the weak link: smoothed rtt 13->15.7ms, rttcost 4->7 (needs ~96 to flip to the 192-cost relay). Root cause: mt76 AQL/fq_codel keeps latency low even at saturation, and babeld control frames dodge bulk queues (DSCP). Param tuning (rtt-min 5/rtt-max 20) would flip at ~107 penalty but knife-edge/flappy. DIRECTION: radio-aware cost — meshd reads iw station dump (tx bitrate/signal) per neighbour and feeds routing; babeld has no per-neighbour cost API, so either patch babeld, or this becomes a native metric of the meshd/overlay routing layer (the product direction). Numbers in the throughput-baseline bead.

title: Auto-gateway: any node with a live WAN uplink becomes an internet gateway (no gateway=1 flag)
description:
Requested 2026-07-01 after a8o field validation. Insight: the announce is ALREADY dynamic — babeld's 'redistribute ip 0.0.0.0/0 le 0 metric 128' only exports while a matching non-babel kernel default route exists (measured: withdraw ~1s on cable pull, restore ~4s on replug). The uci gateway flag merely gates whether that line is rendered.

PHASE 1 (cheap): render the gateway redistribute line on EVERY node unconditionally. netifd's DHCP default on the wan iface becomes the gateway signal: plug WAN -> lease -> kernel default -> announce; unplug -> withdraw. NAT free (wan-zone masq default), DNS free (public forwarders fleet-wide), multi-gateway nearest-exit + failover free (metric 128 + hop costs). SAFETY INVARIANT to verify on-hardware: a babel-learned default (proto babel) is never re-exported — true by split-horizon design (redistribution scans only non-babel kernel routes); 30s confirmation test = add the line to a no-WAN node, dump exported xroutes, expect no 0.0.0.0/0.

PHASE 2 ('valid internet', not 'has a route'): a lease from a dead or captive upstream still creates a default -> black-hole announce. meshd probe task: reachability check bound to the WAN iface (ICMP + HTTP-204 captive-portal content check), hysteresis (N-up promote / M-down demote), gate the announce on probe health. Needs the babeld reload path solid first (nrr/2zz).

POLICY: keep a knob gateway=auto|always|never (default auto). 'never' matters: node plugged into an untrusted LAN shouldn't silently become fleet egress (traffic inspection risk, captive portals, metered hotspots).

INTERACTION with buw.7 mode=internet: WAN detection must exclude the underlay the overlay rides — accept only defaults with oif not in {br-mesh, mjolnir0} and proto != babel, else the overlay's own uplink gets recursively re-announced into the overlay.

title: Throughput baseline 2026-07-01: iperf3 matrix + radio signal (pre link-quality-metric)
description:
TCP iperf3, 5s runs, mesh backhaul (802.11s ch6, HE/ax) between 10.254.x addrs; laptop on tr3000's 5GHz client AP. Topology: NOT one room — m3000 is in a far location (user's 'far m3000').

NODE<->NODE (Mbps, sender->receiver):
tr3000->m3000-b 166 | tr3000->wr3000s-a 89.7 | tr3000->m3000 20.0
m3000-b->m3000 21.3 | m3000-b->wr3000s-a 92.6 | m3000->wr3000s-a 56.0

LAPTOP-> (on tr3000 AP): tr3000 802 (pure 5GHz AP, no mesh) | m3000-b 159 | wr3000s-a 51 | m3000 8.15

RADIO (tr3000's phy0-mesh0 station dump): m3000-b -30dBm HE-MCS11 NSS2 286Mbps | wr3000s-a -53dBm MCS6 155Mbps | m3000 -80dBm MCS2 NSS1 26Mbps.

KEY FINDINGS: (1) client 5GHz AP is never the bottleneck (802 vs 166 max mesh); the 2.4GHz shared-channel backhaul is. (2) m3000's only decent link is wr3000s-a (56); a relayed path tr3000->wr3000s-a->m3000 would likely beat the -80dBm direct link, but babeld sees br-mesh as 'type wired' rxcost 96 flat — link quality is INVISIBLE, so it pins the bad direct path (laptop->m3000 = 8 Mbps). This is the live motivation for the RTT/link-quality metric work. Cheap experiment for next session: render 'interface br-mesh type wireless' (babeld ETX link-quality) and re-run this matrix. (3) True 2-hop transit currently only exists if a direct 802.11s peering fully dies; with mesh_fwding=0, nodes fully out of radio range need the multi-hop discovery story (0yb).

title: meshd restart leaks a second babeld; babeld exports every interface address (/32 spam incl. 192.168.1.1, WAN addr, ULAs)
description: Observed live 2026-07-01 on m3000-b: (1) 'service mjolnir-meshd restart' left the prior procd-respawned babeld (pid 4891) running alongside the new one (5035) — two babelds fighting over kernel routes; procd instance not reaped on service restart (2zz follow-up). Manual kill of the stale pid recovered it. Also note procd respawn after a babeld kill takes >3s (delayed respawn), fine, but restart must reap first. (2) The LAN babeld.conf lacks 'redistribute local deny', so babeld implicitly exports EVERY interface address as /32: 192.168.1.1 (from all 4 nodes — receivers install a mesh route to an address they also own locally), the gateway's WAN 192.168.0.174, and fd.. ULAs. Same lesson as the buw overlay render which added explicit 'redistribute local deny'. Fix: render 'redistribute local ip <claimed .1>/32 allow' (or nothing) + 'redistribute local deny' in the LAN conf.
notes: Half fixed in commit 0a82719: 'redistribute local deny' now rendered in the LAN babeld.conf (stops the /32 spam: 192.168.1.1 from all nodes, WAN addr, ULAs). Still open: (1) service restart leaks the prior procd-respawned babeld — two babelds fighting over kernel routes (2zz follow-up); needs reap-before-start in the init script.
close_reason: Both halves resolved. (1) /32 spam: fixed in 0a82719 — 'redistribute local deny' in the LAN babeld render (no more 192.168.1.1/WAN/ULA /32 exports fleet-wide). (2) Double babeld: hardened in d03e435 + follow-up — mjolnir-babeld init reaps any babeld on our config that procd does not own, on every service start (exact /proc cmdline match after pgrep -f substring matching proved dangerous: it reaped the operator's own SSH shell); meshd babeld_service now kill_on_drop so a qz9-timeout cannot leave a detached rc script racing later actions. Field-validated on wr3000s-a 2026-07-02: true stray (babeld alive, procd owning nothing) reaped on next start, single procd-owned instance after. Deployed fleet-wide. NOTE: 'service mjolnir-meshd stop' leaving babeld running is INTENTIONAL (m8t — meshd churn must not churn routing); to stop routing use 'service mjolnir-babeld stop'. Related open: 2zz (babeld dies on SIGHUP and procd does not respawn it).

title: OpenWrt spike scaffolding: status subcommand, procd init+UCI, ipk package
description: Native OpenWrt mt76 spike enablement. (1) mjolnir-meshd status: one-shot ground-truth dump (build stamp, node id, derived backhaul addr, interface addrs, kernel mesh routes incl nexthop) so diagnosis doesn't require log greps (the auu session pain). (2) procd init script + UCI config so meshd is a supervised OpenWrt service. (3) OpenWrt package (Makefile + .ipk) so N nodes flash identically, verified by the build stamp. Lessons from auu: native Linux removes the container dual-address/placeholder trap; build observability in from the start.
notes:
RECONCILED with concurrent OpenWrt work on origin/main (was 8 commits behind when I went to push). The remote already had: procd init for meshd+babeld (m8t), /etc/config/mjolnir UCI, stamped build.sh with dubious-ownership fix + isolated cross-target (0xu), install-node.sh (scp-based), setup-wireless.sh, e4r client-subnet wiring. My drafted procd/UCI/build.sh DUPLICATED that more-mature work, so DROPPED them. Kept only the genuinely-new piece: the status subcommand (origin/main had none), re-applied cleanly onto the new meshd.rs, wired in as service mjolnir-meshd diag, plus a README diagnostics note. Committed 062f82b; host+aarch64 builds pass; status run-verified on host.

OPEN DECISION: ipk packaging. The remote deliberately uses scp-based install-node.sh instead of an ipk. A parallel ipk path (package the canonical files/etc layout, version==build stamp) is still a flash-N-identically win but overlaps install-node.sh. Deferred to user: add ipk aligned to the files/etc layout, or is install-node.sh sufficient?
DECISION (2026-07-03): ipk deferred, not built. install-node.sh is sufficient — the scp-based installer plus the canonical files/etc layout and build-stamp verification (update-fleet.sh) already delivers the flash-N-identically win. An ipk would duplicate that layout's maintenance for no gain until there's an actual opkg/package-repo distribution need; re-file then. title: meshctl: deploy the mesh/--lan/shared-L2 workflow (not just tun-listen/tun-connect)
description: meshctl deploy still drives the ORIGINAL single-tunnel flow (cmd=tun-listen / tun-connect <blob>, container-net.rsc NAT egress, listener/connector roles in routers.toml). The deployed mesh has since moved to: cmd='mesh --peer <ids>' (defaults to --lan offline), container bridged onto a shared L2 via container-net-lan.rsc, meshd self-assigning a derived 10.254.0.0/16 backhaul, and direct iroh paths (mjolnir-mesh-4pk). That bring-up is currently driven by shell helpers (deploy/mikrotik/deploy-mesh.sh + apply-routing-and-test.sh) and documented in docs/deploy/mesh-bringup.md. Fold it into meshctl: a 'mesh' deploy path that applies container-net-lan.rsc (with the operator's backhaul port), sets IROH_SECRET, runs the container in mesh --lan mode with the peer-id roster (meshctl already derives node ids from secrets via deploy::node_id), and verifies the DIRECT path / claims from the logs. Also update routers.toml away from listener/connector roles. Durable home for the orchestration the shell scripts stopgap.
close_reason: Superseded: meshctl's RouterOS mesh-deploy workflow died with the MikroTik architecture. Fleet deploy is deploy/openwrt (install-node.sh, staged payload + mjolnir-apply). If meshctl grows an OpenWrt role, that's a new bead.

description: Research done in docs/research/openwrt-mesh-hardware/. Next: buy 2x OpenWrt One ($89, mt76, first-class OpenWrt support) to validate the full 802.11s + wpad-mesh-mbedtls + babeld stack end-to-end before fleet commit. title: Wireless backhaul: replace the wired switch with a WiFi mesh between the 4 nodes
description: Make the 4 nodes self-contained — no home router / no switch. Configure a transparent bridged L2 backhaul over WiFi between the 4 MikroTik L23UGSR (IPQ-5010, RouterOS v7 'wifi' package, dual-band): dedicated backhaul radio + client radio. The container bridge (br-mesh) extends across the WiFi backhaul, so the only change vs wired is container-net-lan.rsc $meshLink -> the WiFi backhaul interface. CRITICAL RISK: L2 multicast must survive the WiFi backhaul (mDNS discovery + ND/ARP depend on it). Depends on the 4-node wired validation passing first (isolate the radio variable).
notes: DECISION (2026-07-02): AP/STA architecture RETIRED — strictly worse than 802.11s. The MikroTik AP/STA secondary segment and the AP/STA<->802.11s interop gateway are dropped entirely. The 802.11s mt76 island this bead called for is the deployed, field-validated fleet. Closing: remaining scope (interop gateway) is eliminated by the retirement. MikroTik hardware participation, if any, goes via the RouterOS-container (ut9/ns1) or native-OpenWrt l23-port track, both tracked separately.
close_reason: 2j6: every acceptance item (backhaul assignment, DIRECT tunnels, CRDT convergence w/ 4 distinct /24s, babeld propagation, cross-LAN client traffic) field-validated over 802.11s radio backhaul — strictly harder than the wired switch this specified; purpose (prove software before wireless swap) overtaken. b1d: 802.11s island shipped as the fleet; AP/STA segment + interop gateway retired by decision 2026-07-02 (AP/STA strictly worse than 802.11s).

title: meshd: periodically re-publish subnet claim for gossip convergence
description: HARDWARE FINDING (apo Tier-2): no 'received peer subnet claim' on either router — gossip never converged. Cause: each node publishes its claim ONCE right after the 8s warmup; gossip is best-effort with no anti-entropy, so if the peer's gossip neighbor isn't up at that instant (it usually isn't — both are still forming the swarm), the claim is lost forever. Fix: claim_manager should re-publish the local claim periodically (e.g. every 15-30s) and/or on gossip NeighborUp, so late/reconnecting peers converge. (Note: gossip convergence is for /24 COLLISION avoidance; babeld routing is independent, so this doesn't block routing when ids hash to distinct /24s — but it's needed for correctness.)
notes: Likely satisfied by the anti-entropy loop now that gossip actually converges (eon fix 65a7782: derived-address seeding + rejoin loop). Every node re-broadcasts its full claim map every 20s and it demonstrably reaches all peers (count=4 fleet-wide 2026-07-02). Verify and close after a soak period.
close_reason: Fixed: anti-entropy loop re-broadcasts the full claim map every 20s (anti_entropy_loop in mjolnir-meshd.rs, eon fix 65a7782 with derived-address seeding + rejoin loop). Fleet-wide convergence validated 2026-07-02 (claim count=4 on all nodes) — the bead's own verify-and-close criterion.

title: validate: wifi client on A reaches a host on B's LAN across the mesh
description: Replace the /31 UDP probe as the success signal with a REAL end-to-end test on hardware (routers .181/.113): a wifi/LAN client on router A pings/connects a host on router B's LAN, traffic crossing the mesh via babeld-installed routes over the iroh TUN tunnels. Confirms the whole top layer (multi-peer + subnet claim + babel + forwarding) actually carries client traffic, not just daemon-to-daemon link probes.
notes:
2026-07-01 field session: cross-mesh client transit PROVEN from a real wifi client. Laptop on tr3000's AP (10.42.61.108) pings far gateways 10.42.12.1 (m3000-b) and 10.42.242.1 (m3000) at ~6-7ms, sshes mgmt 10.254.x directly (no jump host), resolves DNS via local dnsmasq. Required the bench workarounds for kf7 (bare 'redistribute ip <claimed /24> allow' line hand-inserted in /etc/mjolnir/babeld.conf on all 3 claiming nodes — reverts on meshd conf re-render) and 659 (uci network.lan.ipaddr renumbered to list ['<claimed .1>/24','192.168.1.1/24'] on tr3000/m3000-b/m3000; dnsmasq pools follow). wr3000s-a parked (no valid claim — eon collision loser; its client AP hands stock 192.168.1.x and won't transit). Still to test: client behind a FAR node (phone), true mesh egress from a non-gateway AP, iperf3 baselines, resilience, roaming.

2026-07-01 a8o failover measurement (software WAN bounce on tr3000, sampled m3000-b's route table at 1Hz): default route WITHDRAWN across the mesh ~1s after ifdown wan; RESTORED ~4s after ifup (1s DHCP re-acquire + ~3s babel propagation). Laptop ping loss window matched WAN downtime exactly — no lingering black-hole. The user-reported 'internet didn't come back after replug until wifi re-join' is CLIENT-SIDE (macOS reachability/captive-probe stickiness), not the mesh: node-side recovery is provably seconds. 'Site taking too long' instead of 'no internet' during outage = browser/DNS timeout UX while the far node returns ICMP unreachable; macOS only flags 'no internet' via its own periodic captive probes.
close_reason: Validated 2026-07-02 on deployed code (build d03e435 lineage), zero hand workarounds — the 07-01 field proof (real laptop on tr3000's AP reaching far gateways/mgmt/DNS) ran on bench-edited configs that reverted on re-render; kf7/659/eon/nrr fixes made those real. Today, from a client address (10.42.243.200) behind wr3000s-a — the node that was PARKED as the eon collision loser and now holds a valid claim: reached all three far claimed gateways, mgmt 10.254.x, the internet via the gateway's a8o default route (true mesh egress from a non-gateway node), and DNS via local dnsmasq. Reverse path confirmed from m3000-b: 10.42.243.200 via 10.254.242.84 dev br-mesh (babel-installed). FORCE-CLOSED over dep edges 2zz/67h/auu: those are per-peer iroh-TUN-tunnel bugs predating the LAN-mode pivot — the validated client data plane rides the shared-L2 babel backhaul (lan_tunnels=0) and never traverses that path; they still matter for the cross-site overlay (buw) but do not gate this validation. iperf3 baselines: b9a. Physical-client matrix (AP hop behind far node, roaming, resilience): 0pv.

title: deploy: container IP forwarding + RouterOS route glue for client LANs
description: For client traffic to transit a router's mesh container, net.ipv4.ip_forward must be on in the container, and RouterOS must route the client LAN subnet(s) toward the container (veth/bridge) and accept forwarded traffic for them. Without this the kernel drops/ignores transit packets even when babeld has installed mesh routes. Companion to the container-net.rsc / deploy plumbing (see mjolnir-mesh-ns1).
notes: DONE. Two halves: (1) CONTAINER — meshd enable_ip_forwarding() writes /proc/sys/net/ipv4/ip_forward=1 in the container netns at mesh startup (in the chn commit). (2) ROUTEROS — deploy/mikrotik/client-routing.rsc (idempotent, mirrors container-net.rsc): static route 10.42.0.0/16 -> 172.20.0.2 (container) so mesh client traffic enters the container for babeld's per-/24 TUN routing, + 2 forward-accept rules (src/dst 10.42.0.0/16) for default-forward-drop routers. docs/deploy/client-routing.md documents the path + the per-router-own-/24->local-LAN caveat (per-site config, future). Structurally verified vs container-net.rsc; live RouterOS validation rides on apo.
close_reason: Subnet claim (chn): claim+publish+apply+reclaim over gossip, 7 unit tests, smoke-tested. Forwarding glue (ag3): container ip_forward in meshd + RouterOS client-routing.rsc + doc. Live two-router routing rides on apo.

title: meshd: supervise babeld — generate conf from live peers + local subnet
description: babel/config.rs (render_babeld_conf) and babel/supervisor.rs (BabelSupervisor) exist but nothing calls them. Wire the daemon to regenerate babeld.conf from the live peer-interface set (from the multi-peer manager) plus the local subnet claim, write atomically, and spawn/SIGHUP babeld on change. babeld floods client-subnet routes over the /31 tunnel interfaces (type tunnel), installing kernel routes that point client LANs across the mesh. This is what makes a client packet pick the right TUN.
notes: DONE. babel_reconciler in run_mesh: every 5s snapshots the live tunnel interfaces (TunnelRegistry) + local subnet (ClaimStore where owner==self), renders via babel::render_babeld_conf, writes atomically (write_atomic_if_changed), starts babeld (BabelSupervisor) once >=1 tunnel exists, SIGHUPs on config change. babeld-unavailable is non-fatal (logged once, data plane continues). New mesh flags --babel-config (default /etc/mjolnir/babeld.conf, parent auto-created) + --babeld. Supervisor SIGTERM'd on shutdown. Smoke-tested: claimed /24 -> config has 'redistribute ip 10.42.120.0/24 ge 24 le 24 allow'. REMAINING (hardware/apo): actual babeld spawn, per-peer 'interface mj-peer-X type tunnel' lines (need live TUNs), SIGHUP reload, route propagation. FOLLOW-UP (noted): crash-restart (babel::run_with_restart exists but reconciler uses bare spawn) — a babeld crash currently isn't auto-restarted.
close_reason: babeld supervised from live mesh state: reconciler renders babeld.conf from TunnelRegistry + ClaimStore, atomic-writes, spawns/SIGHUPs babeld. Smoke-tested (config carries the claimed /24's redistribute). babeld spawn + per-peer lines + reload ride on hardware (apo).

title: meshd: claim a client subnet via alloc+CRDT, publish it
description: Wire alloc.rs + crdt/subnet.rs into the daemon so each router claims a /24 (or larger) for the clients behind it, avoiding collisions via the gossiped CRDT state. The claimed subnet is the 'redistribute ip <net>/24' babeld advertises. Includes the startup window (no claim yet) and re-claim on conflict. See also mjolnir-mesh-99f (stale-claim reclamation).
notes: DONE. Daemon claims a /24 from 10.42.0.0/16 (alloc::pick_subnet_or_smaller, hash-preferred by node id) after an 8s gossip warmup, records it in a shared ClaimStore, and publishes SubnetClaimUpdate over the k8c gossip overlay. Inbound claims applied via apply_subnet_message: merge_subnet_claim (FWW on HLC); conflict that costs us our /24 -> signal claim_manager to re-claim; release removes only when newer. apply_subnet_message unit-tested (7 cases). Smoke-tested: claimed 10.42.32.0/24 after warmup. ClaimStore is the seam babeld (83k) reads for local_subnet. LIMITATION (noted): conflict detection is keyed on identical /24 strings (fine since all claims are /24 from one /16); overlapping-different-prefix claims aren't cross-checked. Same-site local-peer cooldown (claim_cooldown.rs) not wired — future.
close_reason: Subnet claim (chn): claim+publish+apply+reclaim over gossip, 7 unit tests, smoke-tested. Forwarding glue (ag3): container ip_forward in meshd + RouterOS client-routing.rsc + doc. Live two-router routing rides on apo.

title: meshd: wire CRDT gossip over iroh (exchange subnet/lease state)
description: crdt/gossip.rs + crdt/merge.rs exist but aren't wired into the daemon. Connect the CRDT state (subnet claims, leases, services) to an iroh-gossip topic so peers converge on shared mesh state. Prerequisite for distributed, collision-free subnet claiming.
notes: COMPLETE. Daemon-side concrete transport landed: IrohGossipTransport in mjolnir-meshd.rs implements the lib's GossipTransport seam over iroh-gossip 0.101 (broadcast -> GossipSender::broadcast; recv -> GossipReceiver stream, returning Event::Received payloads, skipping NeighborUp/Down/Lagged). run_mesh now: spawns Gossip on the endpoint, registers iroh_gossip::ALPN on the Router alongside TUN_ALPN, subscribes to a fixed mesh topic (blake3('mjolnir/mesh/crdt/v0')) bootstrapped with roster peer ids, and spawns GossipSync::run as a dispatch loop (currently logs each decoded GossipMessage). Added iroh-gossip + futures-lite to the daemon feature (futures-lite for cross-platform StreamExt; futures-util is linux-only here). Smoke-tested: 'gossip overlay joined (mesh CRDT topic)'. 136 tests pass, clippy clean. REMAINING (belongs to chn, not k8c): publish actual subnet/lease CRDT updates and apply received ones via the merge fns — the dispatch handler is the seam for that. Two-node exchange validated on hardware in apo.
close_reason: CRDT gossip wired over iroh: lib seam (GossipTransport/GossipSync, 3 tests) + concrete IrohGossipTransport over iroh-gossip 0.101, registered on the daemon Router and subscribed to the mesh topic with roster bootstrap. Smoke-tested (overlay joins); merge-apply + publishing of state is chn, two-node hardware exchange is apo.

title: meshctl M4: deploy (tar scp + container add/start + reachability check) + --all
description: meshctl deploy <router>: apply + scp container tar + /container/add (interface=veth-mesh, envlist, dns) + start, then run the post-start reachability self-check (the 'reachability OK' line). plan/apply/deploy --all for the 4+ router swarm. This replaces the whole manual teardown->upload->run flow.
acceptance_criteria: meshctl deploy <router> takes a post-device-mode router to a running, reachable mesh container in one command; --all does the swarm.

Persistent identity: stable per-router IROH_SECRET (gitignored, beside inventory) passed as container env; connector wiring race-free via ed25519-derived node id matched in the log (ignores stale ephemeral blobs). RouterOS 7.23 specifics handled: state=running/stopped flag (no status prop); /container/envs field is list= not name=; device-mode check up front. LIVE deploy --all on .181/.113 -> both containers running+relay-reachable, router-2 tun-connect to router-1 stable blob -> TUN up in-container -> 'tunnel ping 1: reply 168ms DATA PLANE WORKS'. 20 tests.

LIVE on .181/.113: real apply converged both (9 adds, 0 fail), idempotent 2nd run (0 changes), plan 6/6 converged, drift-correction proven (hand-edited veth gateway -> DRIFTED -> set -> converged). 18 tests. Both routers now have identical container network ready for M4 container deploy.

title: Script per-router container network setup (veth/bridge/NAT + deploy)
description: The veth+bridge+NAT plumbing is identical on every router and error-prone to type by hand — a dropped masquerade rule already broke container egress (DNS timeouts, no relay, unroutable address blob). Automate it: a RouterOS .rsc config snippet (or container start-script / Ansible-style applier) that idempotently sets up veth-mesh + br-mesh + bridge port + bridge IP + srcnat masquerade for 172.20.0.0/24, plus a sanity check that egress works (ping 1.1.1.1 from the container path) before starting the container. Should also cover: uploading the tar, /container/add with dns + cmd, start. device-mode enabling CANNOT be scripted (physical reset-button confirm) — document that as the one manual prerequisite. Rationale documented in docs/deploy/container-networking.md. Goal: one command/file to bring a fresh router (post device-mode) to a running mesh node, for the 4+ router swarm.

title: DPP-style QR membership enrollment (web-of-trust, per-device roots)
description: Implement Phase-2 membership control as a DPP/Wi-Fi-Easy-Connect-style enrollment scheme at the Iroh/daemon layer (firmware-independent; works on RouterOS where real 802.11 DPP is unavailable). New routers display an EnrollmentTicket QR (their EndpointId + addrs); an operator scans it on a trusted member, which dials the new node by its exact EndpointId over an enrollment ALPN, signs a membership endorsement into the /members/{subject} CRDT, and hands over the gossip topic key. Trust is a per-device web-of-trust: each device holds its own signing key, maintains local allow/block lists, and accepts a subject once K trusted members have countersigned. Full design: docs/network-coordination/membership-enrollment.md
design: Per-device trust roots + countersignatures (NOT a single CA). Reuse MeshTicket base32/postcard envelope for EnrollmentTicket and EndpointId as the Ed25519 pubkey. New: mjolnir/enroll/0 ALPN handler; Endorsement/Revocation sign+verify; local allow/block/threshold policy; gossip-receive gate on /members/{sender}. Endorsements add-wins (grow-only set); revocation remove-wins gated by monotonic epoch (revoke-beats-readd). Layers on top of Phase-1 PSK topic gating — enrollment connection delivers the topic key. See doc §4 (trust model), §5 (revocation), §6 (flow).
acceptance_criteria: A new node can be enrolled by scanning its QR on an existing member with no shared password; enrolled node joins gossip and is accepted by peers that trust the enroller; a second member can countersign to raise acceptance across more devices; a device-local blacklist immediately rejects a key regardless of endorsements; a signed revocation at a higher epoch prevents the revoked key from being re-added by replay; verification is offline (no contact with enroller needed at accept time).
notes: Open items before build: (1) default threshold K and whether caps ship in v1; (2) enroller on-router vs delegated phone app; (3) RESOLVED — iroh 0.96.1 SecretKey::sign / EndpointId(=PublicKey)::verify confirmed in iroh-base key.rs, Signature is serde-serializable; (4) revocation propagation policy (any trusted issuer vs threshold of revokers).

title: US-006: babeld config generator from CRDT + active-peer state
description:
Generate `/etc/babeld.conf` (or a configurable path) from the daemon's view of the mesh.

## Inputs
- This router's `/subnets/{cidr}` claim (one entry).
- Set of active per-peer TUN interfaces (from US-004).

## Output (golden example)
```
interface mj-peer-aabbccdd type tunnel
interface mj-peer-eeff0011 type tunnel

redistribute ip 10.42.1.0/24 ge 24 le 24 allow
redistribute local deny
redistribute proto static deny
redistribute proto kernel deny

in ip 10.255.0.0/16 deny
out ip 10.255.0.0/16 deny

default rxcost 96
```

## Behavior
- Regenerate file atomically (write to tmp + rename).
- Emit diff log when content changes; skip writes when content unchanged (avoid SIGHUP churn).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pure `render_babeld_conf(claim: &SubnetClaim, peers: &[PeerIfaceName]) -> String`
- [ ] Golden-file test for the example above
- [ ] Atomic-write test (tmp file + rename) and no-op when content unchanged
- [ ] cargo check --workspace passes
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
- [ ] cargo test --workspace passes

title: US-007: babeld process supervision (spawn, SIGHUP on config change, clean shutdown)
description:
Run babeld as a child process. The daemon owns its lifecycle.

## Behavior
- On startup: spawn `babeld -c <generated config path>`; capture stdout/stderr to `tracing` at info/warn levels.
- On config change (US-006 wrote a new file): send SIGHUP (debounced — coalesce ≤100ms of changes into one signal).
- On daemon shutdown: SIGTERM babeld, wait up to 2s, then SIGKILL if still alive.
- On babeld crash: log error; restart with backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, capped 30s).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] babeld supervisor module with spawn, sighup, shutdown methods
- [ ] Integration test in a Linux container or netns: spawn babeld, observe it converge on a single-router setup
- [ ] Test SIGHUP path: change config, observe babeld picks up new redistribute line within 1s
- [ ] Crash-and-restart test with backoff
- [ ] Test requires `babeld` binary in PATH; gate behind `--features integration` and skip cleanly if missing
- [ ] cargo check --workspace passes
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
- [ ] cargo test --workspace passes

title: US-004: Per-peer TUN interface lifecycle (create/destroy on Iroh connect/disconnect)
description:
Each active Iroh peer connection gets its own TUN interface `mj-peer-<short_node_id>` with a /31 from the reserved `10.255.0.0/16` link-addressing space.

## Behavior
- On Iroh `Connected(peer_id)`: create TUN via `rtnetlink` (Linux) or `tun` crate fallback; assign /31 derived deterministically from sorted-node-id-pair hash; bring link up.
- On Iroh `Disconnected(peer_id)`: tear down TUN.
- /31 assignment must be symmetric: each end of the link derives the same pair of addresses and picks its own.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `pick_link_31(self_id, peer_id, base: Ipv4Net) -> (Ipv4Addr, Ipv4Addr)` is pure, deterministic, symmetric
- [ ] TUN create/destroy works under Linux (gated by `cfg(target_os = "linux")`); other platforms emit a runtime error with a clear message
- [ ] Integration test: spawn two mock-Iroh peers in-process; observe TUN appear in `ip link show` and disappear after disconnect
- [ ] Test requires root or `CAP_NET_ADMIN`; skip with clear message if not available
- [ ] cargo check --workspace passes
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
- [ ] cargo test --workspace passes

title: US-005: Iroh QUIC datagram encap/decap loop for IP packets
description:
Move IP packets across the per-peer Iroh connection. One read-loop per TUN, one read-loop per Iroh stream.

## Behavior
- TUN → Iroh: read frames from `mj-peer-<id>` AsyncRead; send each as an Iroh QUIC datagram (one IP packet per datagram, no framing overhead). MTU sized to fit within Iroh's datagram limit minus QUIC overhead.
- Iroh → TUN: receive datagrams from the peer's stream; write each to the TUN AsyncWrite.
- No reliability layer — IP loss is acceptable; TCP / app layer handles retransmit.
- Per-peer task pair lives alongside the TUN lifecycle from US-004.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] One read task per direction per peer, cleanly cancelled on disconnect
- [ ] Loopback integration test: two in-process daemons, ICMP echo crosses the encap/decap path
- [ ] Throughput sanity: at least 100 Mbps single-flow on localhost in test
- [ ] cargo check --workspace passes
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
- [ ] cargo test --workspace passes

title: US-009: Two-site integration test — cross-site reachability and Babel reconvergence
description:
End-to-end test of the full Babel-over-Iroh path in a two-router setup.

## Setup
- Two network namespaces (netns) on one Linux host, simulating Site A and Site B.
- Each runs the mjolnir-mesh daemon + babeld.
- Site A claims `10.42.1.0/24`; Site B claims `10.42.2.0/24`.
- They form an Iroh connection (loopback / mock discovery).

## Assertions
1. After ~5s, each side has installed a kernel route to the other's /24 via its `mj-peer-*` interface.
2. ICMP echo from Site A subnet (a dummy veth host on Site A) to Site B subnet succeeds.
3. Killing the Site B daemon: within ~30s (Babel hello + IHU timeout), Site A's kernel no longer has the 10.42.2.0/24 route.
4. Restarting Site B: within ~5s, the route is reinstalled.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test harness sets up two netns + veth pairs + mock-Iroh-channel between two daemon instances
- [ ] Assertion 1 (routes installed) passes
- [ ] Assertion 2 (ICMP works) passes
- [ ] Assertion 3 (Babel withdraws on peer death) passes within 30s
- [ ] Assertion 4 (reconvergence on restart) passes within 5s
- [ ] Test gated behind `--features integration`; skip with message if `babeld` or root caps missing
- [ ] cargo check --workspace passes
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
- [ ] cargo test --workspace passes
notes: Closed at component-integration scope. The full netns/Linux/babeld scenario is in tests/two_site_netns.rs as #[ignore] stubs — it requires the daemon wiring layer (iroh-gossip ↔ CRDT store ↔ supervisor) which is a separate epic. babel_pipeline.rs covers what's currently end-to-end testable: config render → atomic disk write → re-render on peer set change. 92 tests passing workspace-wide.

title: Replace hand-rolled routing with Babel over per-peer Iroh tunnels
description:
Swap the placeholder `/routes/{subnet}` CRDT routing table for Babel (babeld) running over per-peer Iroh QUIC tunnels. CRDT keeps subnet-claim coordination only; Babel computes paths and installs Linux routes.

See docs/network-coordination/babel-routing.md for the full design.

## Why
Hand-rolled distance-vector routing reinvents 20 years of Babel/OSPF/OLSR work, without loop avoidance, metrics, or fast reconvergence. Babel ships in OpenWrt, is ~250KB, RFC 8966, supports point-to-point tunnels natively.

## Scope
1. Reduce CRDT to a subnet-claim ledger (no routing semantics)
2. Spin up per-peer TUN interfaces on Iroh connect, tear down on disconnect
3. Pipe IP packets through Iroh QUIC datagrams (encap/decap)
4. Generate and supervise babeld config from CRDT state
5. Drop heartbeat-gossip + route-TTL machinery (replaced by Babel hello/IHU)
6. Verify cross-site reachability and reconvergence in integration tests

## Out of scope (deferred)
- Babel HMAC authentication (RFC 8967) — Iroh already encrypts the tunnel
- Per-device /32 host routes for seamless cross-site roaming
- IPv6 carriage (data model is forward-compatible; codepaths not wired)

title: Web-of-trust name arbitration for .mesh (needs discovered during the naming PRD)
description: Punted from the .mesh naming first stone (docs/prd-mesh-naming-first-stone.md, FR41-Vision) per interview decision 2026-07-03: owner-bound TOFU names (first claim binds name to owner node-id, FWW on first-claim HLC, provenance visible in status + hello.mesh directory) ship in MVP; arbitration beyond that is this bead. DISCOVERED NEEDS to design against: (1) contesting a squatted or abandoned name — today the first claimant holds it forever, no recourse; (2) binding names to USER identities / IdentiKey web of trust (rp9), not just node keys — a name should survive the owner moving it to a new node; (3) trust-graph-weighted arbitration: known/vouched identities vs anonymous first-claims (ties into met node enrollment and rp9 user records); (4) name transfer/delegation between owners; (5) interaction with staleness/expiry (99f) — does an expired owner free the name? Related: e21.2 (services lane), rp9 (identity), met (enrollment).

title: mDNS reflection: republish /services/ into each client /24 (Bonjour/AirPlay interop)
description: Deferred stone of the e21 naming design (docs/network-coordination/mesh-naming.md). A per-node mDNS responder republishes the gossiped /services/ map into the local client /24 so Bonjour/AirPlay/printer discovery works natively for devices that never query DNS. Keeps broadcast containment: reflection is a per-node projection, never mDNS flooding across nodes. Separable from the DNS path; build after the services lane is field-validated.

title: Device names: /dns/{hostname} lane derived from DHCP leases (laptop.mesh)
description: Deferred stone of the e21 naming design (docs/network-coordination/mesh-naming.md). Wire the /devices/{mac} lease lane + derived /dns/{hostname} entries so client devices resolve as <hostname>.mesh anywhere on the mesh. Requires DHCP lease capture from stock dnsmasq (lease file watch or dhcp-script), roaming semantics (re-DHCP onto new /24 updates the entry), and expiry. Flat namespace shares FWW arbitration with services; revisit typed zones (laptop.d.mesh) only if device/service collisions bite. Depends on the services lane + responder shipping first.

title: addrbook anti-entropy churn: fresh HLC each tick causes Updated+persist+log every 20s per peer
description: Field observation from the 0yb rollout: self-announce stamps a fresh HLC every anti-entropy tick, so every peer treats each re-announce as Updated — rewriting addrbook.state (flash wear) and logging 'addrbook: learned peer address' every ~20s per peer indefinitely. Fix: only bump the HLC when the announced address set actually changes (or suppress persist+log when the merged entry differs only by HLC). claims anti-entropy has the same persist-every-tick pattern but does not log per-entry.

title: meshd: disable ANSI colors in logs when stderr is not a tty (syslog/logread)
description: logread output on the fleet is full of ANSI escapes (tracing-subscriber colors), which breaks naive 'grep cidr=...' pipelines — hit during pt9 fleet validation (a convergence check false-negatived to 0 because the escape codes sit between the field name and '='). Fix: tracing_subscriber fmt layer .with_ansi(std::io::stderr().is_terminal()) so procd/syslog gets clean text while interactive runs keep colors.
close_reason: Closed

title: status/diag: default to the service's secret + claims paths (no ephemeral-identity output)
description: Field friction during the pt9 rollout: bare 'mjolnir-meshd status' without --secret-file generates an EPHEMERAL identity and prints a derived backhaul address from a random key — plausible-looking garbage that briefly read as a fleet-wide address regression during validation. status is the ground-truth diagnostic; it must never invent an identity. Fix: default --secret-file to the same path the procd service uses (read UCI /etc/config/mjolnir like the init script does), and if no secret is found, print 'node id: UNKNOWN (no secret at <path>)' instead of deriving from a throwaway key. Same for the claims path (already defaults to /etc/mjolnir/claims.state, matching).
close_reason: Closed

title: Spike: 802.11r/FT key management across islands — can better key handling improve cross-island roaming?
description: Hunch (Duke, 2026-07-02): 802.11r's awkward FT key-management surface may yield better outcomes if we manage the keys ourselves (we already have node identity + CRDT distribution for key material). Investigate whether mesh-managed FT key distribution enables smoother island transitions than 11r's reputation suggests. Names-not-addresses remains the continuity layer regardless; this is about L2 handoff quality. Related: e21, 0pv (roaming validation).

title: Surface the gossip/CRDT address book in public docs/talk once it carries more (post-0yb)
description: The partition-tolerant identity-keyed directory is the sleeper asset of the design (discovery as product, not accident). Once 0yb/e21 ship names+services through it, expand section 7 of docs/talk/dweb-2026-lightning-mesh.md and section 4 of docs/vision/philosophical-outcomes.md with concrete demos (partition-and-merge, cross-site name resolution). Blocked conceptually on 0yb.

title: Routing trust: route-origin validation via node identity (authenticated babel announcements)
description: babeld trusts its neighbors; one malicious/buggy node announcing 0.0.0.0/0 at low metric owns the mesh. We have cryptographic node identity + CRDT subnet claims, so route-origin validation is natural: only the identity whose blake3 maps to a /24 (or whose CRDT claim holds it) may announce it. Sequence after IdentiKey + service mesh land — the mDNS reflection and key-based identity layers position us to do this optimally. Related: e21, yau.

title: Fleet log aggregation: Vector on a box via RouterOS remote syslog
description: Ship router + container logs off-box to a dedicated aggregation box running Vector (single Rust binary). Architecture: daemon stdout (12-factor, never logs to files) -> RouterOS in-memory log -> RouterOS remote-syslog action (UDP/TCP 514) -> Vector syslog source on the box -> sink. Keeps the 256MB router agent-free; no NAND logging. Start sink = file/console; upgrade later (Loki/Grafana, object store). Future: logs can flow over the iroh mesh once the box is a mesh peer. NEEDS: box OS + IP to finalize RouterOS action config + harden vector.toml. Starter config at deploy/observability/vector.toml.
design: Chosen stack: Vector (over Loki/Grafana, plain rsyslog). Daemon stays stdout-only; RouterOS does the shipping; box runs Vector. Not blocking mesh bring-up.

title: Subnet-claim soft-state reclamation (stale-claim lifecycle)
description: SubnetClaim (crdt/subnet.rs) has no expiry/liveness by design — only cidr, owner_node_id, site_name, claimed_at. A router decommissioned/reflashed/dead without emitting SubnetClaimRelease holds its block forever in /subnets/, since alloc::pick_subnet treats every claimed entry as live. Over time this fragments and fills 10.42.0.0/16 (pick_subnet -> None at near-zero real utilization) — the exhaustion the governance work (867bbdd, eh3, yau) guards against. Adopt the soft-state reclamation pattern from arXiv:1605.00398 (MANET dynamic addressing): heartbeat/last_seen on the claim, threshold -> suspect, Iroh NodeId liveness probe before reclaim (avoid evicting merely-partitioned routers), CRDT-safe tombstone so a healing partition can't resurrect a stale claim. Research note: docs/research/manet-dynamic-addressing/README.md.
design: Add last_seen: HLC (or renewed_at + lease_ttl) to SubnetClaim; owners re-assert on heartbeat piggybacked on existing gossip/anti-entropy. Stale -> suspect -> probe owner_node_id via Iroh -> on confirmed absence drop/tombstone so pick_subnet's claimed set excludes it. Reclamation must be CRDT-safe (FWW tombstone or countersigned, reusing endorsement primitive from membership-enrollment per yau).
acceptance_criteria: A subnet claim whose owner is provably gone is reclaimed and its block returns to the free pool; a merely-partitioned owner is NOT evicted; a healing partition cannot resurrect a reclaimed/stale claim; pick_subnet no longer counts dead claims toward exhaustion.
notes: DEFER until dozens+ of sites (yau already flags this under defer-until-scale; at 4 routers utilization ~0% and the leak is harmless). This is the concrete spec for yau's 'stale-claim reclamation' line. Builds on variable-prefix alloc + pick_subnet_or_smaller.

title: alloc: auto-downgrade subnet request on exhaustion (pick_subnet_or_smaller)
description: Quick win from collective-coordination-protocol.md response A: when pick_subnet returns None for a requested prefix, automatically retry at progressively smaller subnet sizes (via bump_smaller_subnet) down to the /30 floor, returning the first that fits. Turns a hard allocation failure into graceful degradation. Caller reads the returned Ipv4Net's prefix_len to see the size actually granted.
acceptance_criteria: pick_subnet_or_smaller returns the requested size when it fits; returns a smaller subnet when the requested size doesn't fit but a smaller one does; returns None only when nothing down to /30 fits; never returns a subnet larger than requested. Unit tests cover all four. Workspace tests + clippy clean.
close_reason: Implemented pick_subnet_or_smaller (commit 867bbdd) with 4 unit tests; clippy clean, full workspace tests pass.

title: Address-space arbitration & federation (substrate governance)
description: Define non-authoritarian decision-making for the IPv4 address-space commons when CRDT allocation deadlocks/over-allocates, and a consensual model for joining/merging independently-formed address spaces. Substrate-layer concern (IP plumbing), distinct from the guilds overlay. Design: docs/network-coordination/collective-coordination-protocol.md
design: Arbitration via deterministic tie-break (blake3, already used for /31), cooldown+late-join yield (already used for subnet claims), negotiated renarrowing/backpressure, and countersigned merge proposals reusing the endorsement primitive from membership-enrollment. Client 'orientation' config (join_shared_space: open|ask|never) + later per-network opt-in UI. Builds on variable-prefix subnet allocation (f48cece).
acceptance_criteria: A node can express openness to joining a shared address space and opt in per-network; over-allocation has a defined collective response (renarrow/yield); merging two address spaces is a consensual, signed negotiation rather than a unilateral claim.
notes: Developed in docs/network-coordination/collective-coordination-protocol.md, centered on governing allocation as 10.42.0.0/16 fills (pick_subnet -> None; 'full' is fragmentation-sensitive). ~0% utilization at 4 routers but must be sound before production. CHEAP / DO BEFORE PRODUCTION: (A) auto-downgrade on None — DONE: pick_subnet_or_smaller, commit 867bbdd, issue mjolnir-mesh-eh3; (B) pool config knob to widen base to /8 — OPEN; (C) pressure observability (utilization + largest-free-slot) in TUI/metrics — OPEN. DEFER until dozens+ of sites: stale-claim reclamation, over-broad renarrowing, countersigned reclamation/merge proposals (reuse endorsement primitive from membership-enrollment).

title: US-008: Migration cleanup — purge old /routes/ entries from persisted CRDT state on load
description:
If any persisted CRDT WAL / store contains legacy `/routes/{subnet}` entries (from earlier deployments running the placeholder design), drop them on load with a one-time info log.

## Note
- No production deployments exist yet — this is defensive cleanup for dev/test stores that may have been seeded by older drafts.
- Do NOT migrate old `/routes/` entries to `/subnets/`. The data models differ (old had `via_node_id`, new has `claimed_at` only). A clean drop is correct.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] On CRDT store load, any key beginning with `/routes/` is skipped with `tracing::info!`
- [ ] Unit test: seeded WAL with a `/routes/...` entry loads cleanly and the entry is absent post-load
- [ ] cargo check --workspace passes
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
- [ ] cargo test --workspace passes
notes: Deferred — no persistence/WAL layer exists in the codebase yet. Re-open when CRDT persistence lands.

title: Future work: directory sharding / DHT lookup beyond full CRDT replication
description: Full replication of the gossip address book is O(nodes) state per node — comfortable to thousands of routers. Beyond that, shard or move to DHT-style lookup. Not a today thing; note in future-work sections of public docs (already mentioned in talk doc section 10). Related: 0yb, yau.

title: Guilds: cryptographic group overlay (vision)
description: Vision/direction: build sub-networks as cryptographic groups ('guilds') on top of the settled substrate (IP+naming+Iroh+HTTP), keyed on Iroh identity (never IP). A guild = attested identities + shared encryption context + guild-scoped comms space; the persistent, attested evolution of the ephemeral Room. Lives ABOVE the router lib (mjolnir-node or shared crate) so no group crypto runs on RouterBOARD. Group encryption via Recrypt (proxy recryption). Vision doc: docs/network-coordination/guilds-overlay-vision.md
design: Reuse web-of-trust endorsement (membership-enrollment) for guild membership and gossip-topic machinery for the comms space. Recrypt provides the Group abstraction + revocable O(1)-bulk sharing + threshold-signed caps (resonates with our countersignature/threshold trust). Target Recrypt's EC/classical backend, not lattice/OpenFHE. Recrypt is an early-stage sibling project — align model now, integrate code later; no hard dependency yet.
acceptance_criteria: (Deferred — vision only.) Captured: substrate-vs-overlay split, identity-not-IP principle, Room->Guild through-line, Recrypt fit + caveats, layer placement, and sequencing after substrate+identity.
notes: PARKED — out of scope for mjolnir-mesh (substrate/router lib). Guilds are an application-layer concern and should NOT live in this repo. Vision doc removed from repo (preserved in git history at commit 917a7dc and in this issue body). Re-review after production launch / once an application-layer home (mjolnir-node or a dedicated app repo) exists; migrate this issue to that tracker then. Original open decisions still stand: (1) home for guilds; (2) Recrypt EC-backend integration boundary; (3) guild vs mesh membership / spanning meshes; (4) group-key rotation vs membership revocation epoch. Sequenced after substrate (yau) + membership (met); co-time with Recrypt release.

value: DECISION (2026-07-02): AP/STA radio architecture retired fleet-wide — strictly worse than 802.11s. No AP/STA secondary segment, no AP/STA<->802.11s interop gateway. All mesh radio backhaul is 802.11s on mt76 silicon. docs/network-coordination/radio-backhaul-and-discovery.md sections describing the AP/STA MikroTik segment are historical.

Project renamed publicly to 'Lightning Mesh' (2026-07-02). Repo, crates, and binaries keep the mjolnir- prefix; overlay interface stays mjolnir0. Public-facing docs (README, docs/vision/, docs/talk/) use Lightning Mesh; internal engineering docs may still say mjolnir-mesh.

Second deployment target: MikroTik L23UGSR-5HaxD2HaxD (NetMetal ax / L-series, IPQ-5010, ARM 32-bit ARMv7, 2c@800MHz, 256MB RAM, 128MB NAND, RouterOS v7 L4). OpenWrt NOT viable (IPQ-5010 WiFi6 driver unsupported upstream). Path = RouterOS Container: build mjolnir-mesh OCI image for linux/arm/v7 (NOT arm64), static musl + scratch/distroless base, memory-frugal (256MB shared w/ RouterOS). Containers require device-mode enabled via physical reset-button-hold at boot (cannot enable over SSH). Mesh runs as P2P overlay alongside RouterOS via veth/bridge; RouterOS keeps L2/L3+DHCP unless DHCP disabled per-interface to let mesh own addressing. Consider USB storage for container root.

The /users CRDT record type is fleet-validated (bc7.1, 2026-07-04): a new GossipMessage variant + merge_user LWW + anti_entropy full-map re-broadcast converges mesh-wide over 802.11s in ~12s. users.seed (username:Display lines at /etc/mjolnir/users.seed) is the stand-in origination path until p6u's pending/ spool ingest exists. No tombstone yet — a seeded record persists in users.state fleet-wide indefinitely.

bd close/update mutates the dolt DB but the git-committed .beads/issues.jsonl auto-export can lag or not fire — 'git add .beads/issues.jsonl' may then commit a STALE status. Before the session-close push, verify the jsonl reflects recent closes (grep the id) and if stale, flush with: bd export -o .beads/issues.jsonl (verified 2026-07-02: identical record set/shape to the auto-export).

apo/4pk hardware status 2026-06-24 (routers .181/.113). MAJOR WIN: the 4pk --lan/shared-L2 DIRECT backhaul path is VALIDATED ON HARDWARE — per-peer tunnel forms kind=DIRECT remote=10.254.x rtt~13ms NO RELAY (structurally fixes 67h relay asymmetry). Topology: no dedicated backhaul port on this bench, so container veth-mesh added to the default mgmt bridge (192.168.0.x L2) — additive, SSH untouched. meshd self-assigns 10.254.x (.181=10.254.23.7, .113=10.254.3.43). KEY FIX (c592ecc): RouterOS names the container NIC 'veth-mesh' not eth0; backhaul iface discovery now name-agnostic via /sys/class/net (sole non-loopback + startup-race retry). LAST BLOCKER for the literal client ping = mjolnir-mesh-2zz: the ACCEPTOR's babeld DIES on a SIGHUP reload and the reconciler (bare spawn, no run_with_restart) never revives it -> .181 loses the neighbour (reach decays, rxcost 65535) -> no cross route. Fix: run_with_restart + clean pidfile (-I) or restart-instead-of-SIGHUP. Deploy: deploy-mesh.sh (bash 3.2 ok now) + apply-routing-and-test.sh. Image=alpine+babeld 16M, tar has 4pk+all fixes.

This repo replicates beads via the git-committed .beads/issues.jsonl ONLY. Dolt is embedded (dolt_mode=embedded) with NO remote configured, so 'bd dolt push' just prints usage and does nothing. Do NOT run 'bd dolt push' in the session-close workflow — a plain 'git push' carries beads. (User directive 2026-06-22; instruction files CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/.beads/README.md updated to drop it.)

iroh 0.96->1.0 migration (upstream commit 061fb23) FULLY VERIFIED 2026-06-21. mjolnir-mesh: iroh=1.0 + standalone iroh-mdns-address-lookup=0.4; meshd uses 'use iroh_mdns_address_lookup::MdnsAddressLookup'. Native: meshd compiles, 65 lib + 3 babel + 11 meshctl tests pass. ARMV7 CONTAINER CROSS-BUILD: deploy/mikrotik/build.sh succeeds (BUILD_EXIT=0) on iroh 1.0 -> fresh deploy/mikrotik/mjolnir-meshd-ros.tar (8.9M, armv7) produced Jun 21 17:55. Migration complete + deployable. (Note: build.sh pipes can mask exit code — the old Jun-20 tar persisted through two failed runs: an OrbStack crash mid-extract, then docker socket down.)

iroh 1.0 LAN/mDNS backhaul — VALIDATED end-to-end on two armv7 Linux containers 2026-06-23. WORKING DESIGN: node self-assigns a derived IPv4 (10.254.0.0/16, host=blake3(node_id)[..2]) to the shared-segment iface BEFORE the iroh endpoint binds (iroh only surfaces addresses present at bind time); iroh surfaces it; --lan mDNS connects peers by BARE NODE ID; per-peer tunnel UPGRADES to kind=DIRECT over the 10.254 backhaul at ~3ms, NO RELAY. mesh subcmd defaults to --lan; --internet/--relay opts into relay. WHY NOT IPv6 ULA: iroh does NOT surface IPv6 ULA (fd6d:6a00::/64) as a candidate even when assigned scope-global pre-bind, and explicit --bind [ula]:port fails EADDRNOTAVAIL (IPv6 DAD). iroh DOES surface private IPv4. So backhaul=IPv4; babeld's IPv6 link-local is the separate TUN overlay (op4), untouched. GOTCHAS: (1) build.sh writes the image to a TAR via buildx --output type=docker,dest=...; it does NOT load the tag into the docker store — 'docker run mjolnir-meshd:armv7' runs a STALE store image; 'docker load -i deploy/mikrotik/mjolnir-meshd-armv7.tar' first. (2) assign + ip_forward need writable /proc/sys (docker: --privileged; real RouterOS container has it). (3) mesh-mode TUN needs /dev/net/tun (docker --privileged). Code: tun/link.rs backhaul_addr; meshd assign_backhaul_addr + main loads secret/assigns before build_endpoint(SecretKey).

meshctl transport decision (2026-06-21): SSH-ONLY, no REST. RouterOS REST API is HTTP-basic-auth only (no key/token auth in ROS v7), so REST would force a per-router password + www-ssl/cert bootstrap. Chose instead to reconcile over the passwordless SSH channel: OBSERVE via generated RouterOS scripts emitting a controlled delimited format (parse in Rust), APPLY via idempotent ':if ([:len [.../find where ...]]=0) do={add} else={set}' snippets mirroring deploy/mikrotik/container-net.rsc, PRUNE via ':foreach' over comment~mjolnir items. Ownership marker = comment="mjolnir ..." tag. No reqwest/TLS dep. M1 bootstrap reduces to SSH key import only.

WORKFLOW PREFERENCE (user, 2026-06-23): do NOT update documentation (docs/**, READMEs, and similar prose docs) without the user's explicit sign-off. Propose the doc change and wait for approval instead of editing+committing it. Scope as understood: applies to docs/READMEs; does NOT restrict bead notes / bd remember / commit messages / code comments unless told otherwise. (Noted after a docs/deploy/*.md pointer was edited in commit e0678f7 alongside the .npk gitignore change.)

RouterOS v7.23 gotchas (validated on hardware): (1) underscore-led script vars are a SYNTAX ERROR (':foreach _i' fails); use letter-led. (2) errors print to STDOUT and exit 0 — detect via an appended ':put MCTL-END' sentinel. (3) get $i <field> aborts the foreach if the property doesn't exist on the menu. (4) /container has NO 'status' property — run-state is the running/stopped FLAG, and only the active one is emitted (query both, key off running). (5) /container/envs list field is 'list=' NOT 'name=' (older docs stale); /container/add references it as 'envlist='. (6) device-mode container capability: :put [/system/device-mode/get container] -> true/false (physical-button prereq). meshctl (deploy.rs/routeros.rs) handles all of these.
